messages.author: Piney
messages.text: I just swapped over from meshtastic to meshcore and realised there's no one near me.
messages.content_html: I just swapped over from meshtastic to meshcore and realised there's no one near me.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Fish
messages.text: Give it time. The mesh will grow.
messages.content_html: Give it time. The mesh will grow.

messages.author: Piney
messages.text: Well I see someone on the internet map at Kingscote, Kangaroo Island 144km away and I'm upgrading one of my repeaters anyway. Hopefully it's enough to reach them. Very exciting for a NERD like me.
messages.content_html: Well I see someone on the internet map at Kingscote, Kangaroo Island 144km away and I'm upgrading one of my repeaters anyway. Hopefully it's enough to reach them. Very exciting for a NERD like me.

messages.author: Bullfrog VK3TFH
messages.text: We are working on getting links into SA but need to get a few more interested in hosting a repeater. If you can get a yagi antenna on your repeater you might be surprised who you find as not all sites are on the internet map.
messages.content_html: We are working on getting links into SA but need to get a few more interested in hosting a repeater. If you can get a yagi antenna on your repeater you might be surprised who you find as not all sites are on the internet map.
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Piney
Well I see someone on the internet map at Kingscote, Kangaroo Island 144km away and I'm upgrading one of my repeaters anyway. Hopefully it's enough to reach them. Very exciting for a NERD like me.

messages.author: Piney
messages.text: I have a shiny new 2.4ghz yagi (with some mounting parts missing) that was being put in the bin at work. I may try some experiments with that. I also have one heltec v3 solar repeater with a 915mhz (advertised frequency but who knows really) 6dbi omni all tested and keeping 2x 18650s above 80% even in poor weather. I'm going to put that up a tree at the highest point within 5km from my place. Then waiting on the weather proof box and a couple of parts for my heltec v4 and 12dbi omni at mt gawler which should get a fair coverage of the north of Adelaide.
messages.content_html: I have a shiny new 2.4ghz yagi (with some mounting parts missing) that was being put in the bin at work. I may try some experiments with that. I also have one heltec v3 solar repeater with a 915mhz (advertised frequency but who knows really) 6dbi omni all tested and keeping 2x 18650s above 80% even in poor weather. I'm going to put that up a tree at the highest point within 5km from my place. Then waiting on the weather proof box and a couple of parts for my heltec v4 and 12dbi omni at mt gawler which should get a fair coverage of the north of Adelaide.
messages.reply_html:
Bullfrog VK3TFH
We are working on getting links into SA but need to get a few more interested in hosting a repeater. If you can get a yagi antenna on your repeater you might be surprised who you find as not all sites are on the internet map.

messages.author: Piney
messages.content_html: Maybe something like this...

messages.author: Bullfrog VK3TFH
messages.text: If you are looking to put out solar repeaters the best units are seeed xiao nrf52840 and sx1262 lora. idle current is <15mAh, about 4X less than heltec. I would keep the heltec for your mobile units
messages.content_html: If you are looking to put out solar repeaters the best units are seeed xiao nrf52840 and sx1262 lora. idle current is <15mAh, about 4X less than heltec. I would keep the heltec for your mobile units
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.text: I'll have to give it a few weeks so the wife doesn't notice

their voltage reading work over the air.

messages.author: Piney
messages.text: Thats a nice part of the world
messages.content_html: Thats a nice part of the world

messages.author: Bullfrog VK3TFH
embeds_html:
Wio-SX1262 for XIAO
This is a high-performance LoRa module specifically designed for the XIAO series development boards. Based on the Semtech SX1262 chip, it supports global LoRa bands (862-930MHz).

messages.author: Piney
messages.text: Getting from Port Fairy to Adelaide is going to be no mean feat. I wish you luck.
messages.content_html: Getting from Port Fairy to Adelaide is going to be no mean feat. I wish you luck.

messages.author: Bullfrog VK3TFH
messages.text: Their is Meshcore in Mount Gambier, Robe. Just need some good hills and Meshcore users will to put up a repeater. Do you watch the Melbourne channel on Meshcore? plans are a foot to connect Tas with a single link.
messages.content_html: Their is Meshcore in Mount Gambier, Robe. Just need some good hills and Meshcore users will to put up a repeater. Do you watch the Melbourne channel on Meshcore? plans are a foot to connect Tas with a single link.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: The Jess + E
messages.text: Hey, I have 5 nodes that i've made with the aforementioned nrf52840 + SX1262. 1 is a router with 2x 18650s on my roof (i live on a hill). With this i am able to easily service my whole town (of 6000 people lol.. I'm the only nerd). I am keen to help build the mesh, happy to coordinate with you all, currently setting on Victoria's settings since I'm quite close to the border but can always change them, let me know (edited)
messages.content_html: Hey, I have 5 nodes that i've made with the aforementioned nrf52840 + SX1262. 1 is a router with 2x 18650s on my roof (i live on a hill). With this i am able to easily service my whole town (of 6000 people lol.. I'm the only nerd). I am keen to help build the mesh, happy to coordinate with you all, currently setting on Victoria's settings since I'm quite close to the border but can always change them, let me know (edited)

messages.author: The Jess + E
messages.text: Do you know if anyone is still using meshcore in mount gambier / robe, i dont see any recent nodes on the map? Might be worth a drive to go find out

Bullfrog VK3TFH
Their is Meshcore in Mount Gambier, Robe. Just need some good hills and Meshcore users will to put up a repeater. Do you watch the Melbourne channel on Meshcore? plans are a foot to connect Tas with a single link.
